claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child or children you're claiming for.**

**How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

Please tell me their age(s):
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children of different ages, please let me know each of their ages and I can assess eligibility for each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for your help. I have one child, Alex, who is 15 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me continue the assessment for Alex, who is 15 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me move to the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

- **Yes** - Alex works fewer than 24 hours per week, or doesn't have a paid job
- **No** - Alex works 24 hours or more per week

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't have a paid job at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, so Alex isn't working at all, which means they meet this requirement. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

**Is Alex NOT receiving any of the following benefits in their own name?**
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, Alex is eligible if they are **not** claiming these benefits themselves.

- **Yes** - Alex is NOT receiving ESA or Universal Credit in their own right
- **No** - Alex IS receiving one of these benefits in their own right

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - Alex isn't receiving any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, Alex isn't receiving any benefits in their own name. Let me continue the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

**Is Alex currently doing an apprenticeship?**

This is important because some types of apprenticeships can affect eligibility:

- **Not in an apprenticeship** - Alex is not doing any apprenticeship
- **Foundation Apprenticeship in Wales** - This is approved and still qualifies for Child Benefit
- **Other apprenticeships** - These would disqualify from Child Benefit, including:
  - Any apprenticeship in England (Intermediate, Advanced, Higher, or Degree)
  - Modern Apprenticeships in Scotland
  - Apprenticeships in Wales (except Foundation level)

Is Alex in an apprenticeship? If so, what type and where?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't doing an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check about your residency status.**

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You only need to meet **ONE** of these to qualify:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You are a resident in the UK

2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status AND are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)

4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status AND are actively looking for work (can claim as a jobseeker for up to 91 days)

5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status AND can support yourself financially (and are NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)

6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status AND are studying with sufficient financial resources

7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside

8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm normally resident here.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, you meet the residency requirement as you normally live in the UK. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next, I need to check if you're responsible for Alex.**

**Do you meet any of the following?**

You only need to meet **ONE** of these:

1. **Living with Alex** - Alex lives with you

2. **Contributing to Alex's upkeep** - You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care. This includes things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents - whether weekly, monthly, or as lump sum payments.

3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments for Alex under a court order or agreement, and the amount equals or exceeds the Child Benefit payment

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, Alex lives with you so you meet the responsibility requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

- **No other claimant** - Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- **Other claimant, but agreed transfer** - Someone else has been claiming, but you've both agreed that you should now be the one to claim
- **Disputed** - There's a disagreement about who should claim Child Benefit for Alex

Which of these describes your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, there's no other claimant for Alex. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex's living situation:**

**Where does Alex currently live?**

- **Normal living** - Alex lives at home with you in a normal family setting
- **In hospital** - Alex is currently staying in hospital
- **In care** - Alex is in local authority care
- **Fostered** - Alex is being fostered

Which of these best describes Alex's current situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me in a normal family setting.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, Alex lives at home with you. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words:
- **Yes** - The local council is NOT contributing to Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs
- **No** - The local council IS paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance

Which applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - the local council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! You've met all the eligibility requirements. Let me record the outcome for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

### Summary of Your Eligibility:
✅ Alex is under 16 years old  
✅ Alex is not working 24+ hours per week  
✅ Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right  
✅ Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship  
✅ You normally live in the UK  
✅ Alex lives with you  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex  
✅ The local council is not paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance  

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge** - if you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may have to pay some or all of the benefit back through tax
5. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

📎 **Apply here:** [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Age: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16 and automatically qualifies for the age requirement. (2) Work status: Alex does not have a paid job, so meets the requirement of working less than 24 hours per week. (3) Benefits: Alex is not receiving ESA or Universal Credit in their own right. (4) Apprenticeship: Alex is not in any apprenticeship. (5) Claimant residency: The claimant normally lives in the UK. (6) Responsibility: Alex lives with the claimant at home. (7) No other claimant: Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. (8) Living situation: Alex lives at home in a normal family setting. (9) Council payments: The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 15), who lives with them in a normal family setting in the UK. All eligibility criteria have been met, and there are no disqualifying factors such as other claimants, council payments, apprenticeships, or the child receiving benefits in their own right.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment
